commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: Shall raise up us also . . . and shall present us with you. Shall present us with you in glory. He says out of modesty, "shall present us with you," not "you with us," because the Corinthians were the cause and object of his preaching, and so also of his glory.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Again, he fills them with lofty thoughts , that they may not hold themselves indebted to men, I mean to the false Apostles. For the whole is of God Who wills to bestow upon many, so that the grace may appear the greater. For your sakes, therefore, was the resurrection and all the other things. For He did not these things for the sake of one only, but of all.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Once again, Paul fills the Corinthians with lofty thoughts, so that they may not feel indebted to the false apostles.

commentaries.author: Polycarp of Smyrna
commentaries.content: He who raised Jesus from the dead will raise us also if we do his will and walk in his commandments and love the things which he loved, abstaining from all unrighteousness, covetousness, love of money, evil speaking and false witness.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content:
What fellowship has light with darkness, life with death?.
Then he has clearly predicted the resurrection of the flesh.
